step1.rightsContext: Under UK and EU passenger rights law (UK261 and EC 261/2004), you may be entitled to compensation for delays, cancellations, and denied boarding. Upload your boarding pass to check eligibility — free and no win, no fee.
step1.uploadHint: Drag & drop or click to upload (PDF, JPG, PNG)

faq.pageIntro: Everything you need to know about claiming under UK261 and EC 261/2004, our no win no fee service, and how we protect your data.

default.question: Is it safe to upload my boarding pass?
default.answer: Yes, absolutely. Your boarding pass is encrypted and stored securely. We only use the information necessary to process your claim and never share it with third parties.

default.question: Does the assistant handle everything?
default.answer: The assistant helps speed up the process, but your claim is also supported by real people. Our team can review your case, contact the airline and help manage the claim from start to finish.

default.question: How much compensation can I receive?
default.answer: For UK flights, UK261 provides up to £520 per passenger (£220 for flights under 1,500 km, £350 for 1,500–3,500 km, and £520 over 3,500 km). For eligible EU flights, EC 261/2004 provides €250, €400, or €600 on the same distance bands.

default.question: What does 'no win, no fee' mean?
default.answer: It means you pay nothing if we don't win your claim. We only charge a success fee when we successfully recover compensation on your behalf.

default.question: What does the assistant do?
default.answer: Our assistant analyzes your boarding pass, checks eligibility under UK261 and EU Regulation EC 261/2004, and prepares all necessary documentation to file your compensation claim.

default.question: Can I speak to a real person?
default.answer: Yes! Our human support team is available to assist you throughout the claim process. You can reach us via chat, email or phone.

default.question: How much does it cost?
default.answer: There is no upfront cost. Compensall works on a no win, no fee basis, which means we only charge a fee if your claim is successful.

default.question: Can I delete my data?
default.answer: Yes. You can request deletion of your personal data at any time. Contact our support team and we will process your request within 30 days in compliance with GDPR.

uk.question: What is UK261 and how is it different from EC 261?
uk.answer: After Brexit, the UK retained passenger rights through UK261, which mirrors EU Regulation EC 261/2004 for flights departing from the UK. UK261 sets compensation in pounds (£220, £350, or £520) while EC 261 uses euros (€250, €400, or €600) for eligible EU departures.

uk.question: Does UK261 apply to my flight?
uk.answer: UK261 generally applies to flights departing from UK airports. EC 261/2004 may apply to flights departing from EU airports, and in some cases to flights arriving in the EU on EU or UK carriers. The regulation that applies depends on your route and airline.

uk.question: Can I claim for a delayed flight under UK261?
uk.answer: Yes. If your flight arrived at its final destination more than three hours late, and the delay was not caused by extraordinary circumstances, you may be entitled to compensation under UK261 or EC 261/2004, depending on which regulation applies to your journey.

uk.question: How long do I have to claim flight compensation in the UK?
uk.answer: Time limits depend on where your claim is pursued. In England and Wales, you generally have six years to bring a claim. We recommend starting as soon as possible while flight records and evidence are easier to obtain.

about.title: About us
about.subtitle: We help passengers claim the compensation they are owed with our assistant and human support every step of the way.
about.missionP1: Compensall makes flight compensation simple. Airlines often delay responses, reject valid claims, and make the process stressful, so too many passengers never receive what EU regulation EC 261/2004 entitles them to.
about.missionP2: We take care of everything: paperwork, negotiations with the airline, and pursuing your case when needed. Upload your boarding pass, confirm your details, and let our team fight for your compensation on a no win, no fee basis.
whyChoose: No win, no fee: you pay nothing unless we succeed
End-to-end handling from eligibility check to airline follow-up
Predictable success fee. We are not a law firm
GDPR-first approach to your personal data
Up to €600 per passenger on eligible flights
Trusted by thousands of travellers across Europe

prices.noWinNoFeeBody: Checking eligibility and using our website is free. If you appoint us to pursue compensation, our fee applies only when your claim is successful, as set out in our <noWinNoFeeAgreement>No Win, No Fee Agreement</noWinNoFeeAgreement>.
prices.successFeeDescription: When we recover compensation on your behalf, we deduct a success fee before transferring the balance to you. You receive at least 75% of the recovered amount before VAT adjustments.
prices.uk261Intro: For UK departures, fixed compensation under UK261 depends on flight distance:

cta.subtitleLine2: Fast, secure and simple from the first upload.

passengerRights.rightsIntro: Air passengers in the UK and EU are protected when flights are delayed, cancelled, or disrupted through no fault of their own.

uk261Card.title: UK261 — UK departures
uk261Card.body: After Brexit, the UK retained passenger rights through UK261. It applies to flights departing from UK airports and sets fixed compensation in pounds: £220, £350, or £520 depending on distance.

ec261Card.title: EC 261/2004 — EU departures
ec261Card.body: EU Regulation EC 261/2004 continues to protect passengers on eligible flights departing from EU airports, with compensation of €250, €400, or €600 on the same distance bands.

passengerRights.rightsDetailP1: If your flight is delayed by three hours or more at arrival, cancelled with short notice, or you are denied boarding against your will, you may be entitled to fixed compensation. You may also have rights to care, rerouting, and refunds depending on the circumstances.
passengerRights.rightsDetailP2: Missed connections and airline strikes can also qualify when the disruption is within the airline's control. Compensall helps you check eligibility, prepare your claim, and pursue compensation with the airline on a no win, no fee basis.
passengerRights.eligibilityIntro: You may be eligible if all of the following apply to your journey.

ukEuDeparture.title: UK or EU departure
ukEuDeparture.body: Your flight departed from a UK airport (UK261) or an EU airport (EC 261/2004), or meets other eligibility rules for your route.

significantDisruption.title: Significant disruption
significantDisruption.body: You arrived at your final destination three or more hours late, or your flight was cancelled or you were denied boarding.

airlineResponsibility.title: Airline responsibility
airlineResponsibility.body: The disruption was not caused by extraordinary circumstances outside the airline's control, such as severe weather or air traffic restrictions.

validBooking.title: Valid booking
validBooking.body: You checked in on time and hold a valid booking for the affected flight.

withinTimeLimits.title: Within time limits
withinTimeLimits.body: You start your claim within the applicable time limit — generally up to six years in England and Wales.

passengerRights.ec261Note: For eligible EU flights, EC 261/2004 provides €250, €400, or €600 on the same distance bands.

220.label: Flights up to 1,500 km

250.desc: EC 261/2004 compensation for shorter eligible flights.
